title: Fermented Aerated Quick-Fix Jamaican Cheddar Bao
description: A quick-fix, fermented, and aerated Jamaican dish featuring mature cheddar and plant-based-friendly ingredients, served in a sashimi-cut bao. This recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner or lunch.

ingredients.name: active dry yeast
ingredients.notes: instant yeast available as a substitute

instructions: Combine sourdough starter and all-purpose flour in a mixing bowl and mix until just combined.
Add mature cheddar cheese, plant-based milk, maple syrup, jamaican jerk seasoning, and olive oil to the bowl and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Add active dry yeast, ginger, coconut flakes, water, and pilsner yeast (if using) to the bowl and mix until the dough comes together in a sticky ball.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a warm place for 4-5 hours, or until it has doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on how large you want your baos to be.
Shape each piece into a ball and then use your thumbs to create a hole in the center of each ball, stretching the dough out to form a bao.
Place the baos on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each bao.
Bake the baos for 15-20 minutes, or until they are golden brown and cooked through.
While the baos are baking, make the aoili by mixing together store-bought or homemade aoili, sliced jalapeno, and a pinch of salt.
Once the baos are done, let them cool for a few minutes before slicing them in half lengthwise and serving with the aoili.
Slice the mature cheddar cheese into thin strips and use them as a garnish for the baos, along with any additional toppings you like.
Serve the fermented aerated quick-fix Jamaican cheddar baos warm, garnished with sliced jalapeno and additional toppings as desired.
